svg.title: Spherical coordinates and modal bookkeeping
svg.desc: Cleaner SPHMS schematic showing a sphere of radius a, the polar angle theta, incident-wave direction, and the separation between exterior and interior radial basis functions.

text.style: font-style:italic;font-variant:normal;font-weight:normal;font-stretch:normal;font-size:20px;line-height:normal;font-family:Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;-inkscape-font-specification:'Georgia, Times New Roman, serif, Italic';font-variant-ligatures:normal;font-variant-caps:normal;font-variant-numeric:normal;font-variant-east-asian:normal;fill:#223127;fill-opacity:1

font-style:italic;font-variant:normal;font-weight:normal;font-stretch:normal;font-size:24px;font-family:Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;-inkscape-font-specification:'Georgia, Times New Roman, serif, Italic';font-variant-ligatures:normal;font-variant-caps:normal;font-variant-numeric:normal;font-variant-east-asian:normal

svg.text: The spherical surface keeps the angular basis matched, so each n-mode remains decoupled.
SPHMS differs by boundary condition, not by a change in the underlying spherical coordinate geometry.
